behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in the rename category, not double-counted as added+deleted.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the Git subprocess + parser handoff: rename detection depends on real `git` status/diff behavior and flags, and mocked output can miss bugs where the CLI invocation returns add/delete instead of rename. Not Agentic/Workflow because no AI step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows the file under `renamed` with no corresponding increment in `added` or `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash processing and binary changes are counted.
behaviors.justification: This behavior can fail due to real subprocess output/encoding from Git binary diff metadata; in-process mocks often hide decode/stream edge cases. Not Agentic/Workflow because behavior is deterministic CLI processing with no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file, modify it with different binary content, run the CLI, and assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file change in counts.

behaviors.description: On a repository with no commits, the tool returns an empty report instead of an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is a real Git subprocess/state case (exit codes/messages) that module-only tests can misrepresent. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML call and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new Git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, and assert successful exit plus an empty structured report on stdout (no stack trace/error).

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s actual change history.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness across real repo history, Git subprocess queries, and report aggregation. Mocking Git responses can hide command/flag mismatches or history interpretation bugs. Not Agentic/Workflow since no model-driven decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history containing known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI for that range, and assert exact equality between expected counts and output counts.

behaviors.description: Results are identical when analyzing staged versus unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git index/worktree semantics plus CLI subprocess interaction; staged/unstaged distinctions are OS/Git stateful behavior that mocks often oversimplify. Rejected higher levels: no AI output quality check and no chained agentic stages in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, prepare equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI in both modes/contexts, and assert the structured stdout reports are byte-for-byte identical.
